full_response:
Step-by-step derivation

1. Geometry of the cone  
   • Axis of symmetry: the z–axis.  
   • Apex at the origin (0,0,0).  
   • Base is a circle of radius r that lies in the plane z = h.  
   • Therefore, at a given height z (0 ≤ z ≤ h) the cross-section is a circle whose radius ρ(z) grows linearly from 0 at the apex to r at the base:

        ρ(z) = (r/h) z .

2. Choice of parameters  
   • θ : the usual azimuthal angle around the z–axis  
          θ ∈ [0, 2π).  
   • z : the height measured along the z–axis  
          z ∈ [0, h].

3. Cartesian coordinates of a point on the cone  
   Using polar coordinates in the xy–plane and the expression for ρ(z):

        x = ρ(z) cosθ = (r/h) z cosθ ,  
        y = ρ(z) sinθ = (r/h) z sinθ ,  
        z = z .

4. Complete parametric description  
   The 2-dimensional surface of the cone is therefore

        (x(θ,z), y(θ,z), z(θ,z)) =
        ( (r/h) z cosθ , (r/h) z sinθ , z ) ,

   with parameter ranges

        0 ≤ z ≤ h , 0 ≤ θ < 2π .

\boxed{(x,y,z)=\bigl(\tfrac{r}{h}\,z\cos\theta,\;\tfrac{r}{h}\,z\sin\theta,\;z\bigr),\qquad
       0\le z\le h,\;0\le\theta<2\pi} 

Confidence: 92%
